standard_description: Use the process of factoring and completing the square in a quadratic function to show zeros, extreme values, and symmetry of the graph, and interpret these in terms of a context.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Find the roots by factoring each of the following quadratic equations.
Additionally, for each equation, identify:
If the function has symmetry around the
y
-axis
If the root is a double root and the same as the vertex
The
y
-intercept
The vertex
a.
$${3x^2-27=0}$$
b.
$${2x^2+7x-15=0}$$
c.
$${4x^2-10x+25=0}$$

Problem 1
A business is marketing its product and has collected data on sales and prices for the past few years. The company, through careful modeling, has determined that their profit is dependent upon the selling price of the item and that it follows a quadratic model.
The function below describes the profit,
$$P$$
, dependent upon the selling price,
$$s$$
, of its product.
$$P(s)=-20s^2+1,400s-12,000$$
a.  Write the function in intercept form and identify the roots.
b.  Explain what the roots mean in context of the situation.
c.  Write the function in vertex form and identify the vertex.
d.  Explain what the vertex means in context of the situation.
Problem 2
The height,
$$h$$
, in feet, of a projectile launched upward from a bridge is given by
$$ h=-16t^2+16t+96$$
, where
$$t$$
is time in seconds.
Write the equation in vertex form.
How long does it take for the projectile to reach maximum height?
What is the maximum height of the projectile?

Problem 1
A ball is thrown straight up from the top of a
$${48}$$
-foot tall building with an initial speed of
$${32}$$
feet per second. The height of the ball as a function of time can be modeled by the function
$$h(t)=-16t^2+{32}t+{48}$$
. Below is a graph and table of the function.
###IMAGE0###
a. What are the solutions to this function? Interpret each solution in context of the situation.
b. What is the vertex of this function? Interpret the vertex in context of the situation.
Problem 2
The table below represents the value of Andrew’s stock portfolio, where
$$V$$
represents the value of the portfolio in hundreds of dollars and
$$t$$
is the time in months since he started investing. Answer the questions that follow based on the table of values.
###TABLE0###
Assuming this data is quadratic, how much did Andrew invest in his stock initially? Explain how you arrived at this answer.
What is the maximum value of his stock and how long did it take to reach the maximum value?
After approximately how many months is Andrew’s stock portfolio valued at $0?
How fast is Andrew’s stock value decreasing between months 10 and 12? Find a two-month interval where the average rate of change is faster than it is between months 10 and 12 and explain why.
Problem 3
John is building a small rectangular pen for chickens. He wants the length to be 6 more feet than twice the width.
If the area of the pen is 36 square feet, what are the dimensions of the pen in feet?

Problem 1
A rectangular garden has a width of
$$x$$
feet. The length of the garden is
$$4$$
feet longer than the width. Around the garden there is a pathway that measures
$$3$$
feet across the path.
###IMAGE0###
The expression below represents the area of the pathway, not including the garden.
$$(x+6)(x+10)-x(x+4)$$
Explain what each part of the expression represents in context of the situation.
a.
$$x$$
b.
$$x+4$$
c.
$$x+10$$
d.
$$x(x+4)$$
e.
$$(x+6)(x+10)$$
Problem 2
An artist has a strip of wood
$$8$$
feet long to make a large rectangular picture frame. The possible dimensions of the frame that the artist can create can be represented as
$$x$$
and
$$4-x$$
, as seen in the diagram below.
###IMAGE1###
What is the largest area the artist can frame with the
$$8$$
feet of wood?

A bakery sells small cakes for
$${ {{$1}}0}$$
each. At this price, the bakery typically sells
$${100}$$
cakes per week.
The owner of the bakery wants to increase the price of the cake in order to maximize revenue. She determines that for each
$${{$1}}$$
increase in price, she sells
$$5$$
fewer cakes per week.
a.  Which function represents the weekly revenue,
$${R(x)}$$
, the owner of the bakery can expect to earn from the cakes based on
$$x$$
increases in price by
$${{$1}}$$
.
i.
$${R(x)}=(10-x)({100}+5x)$$
ii.
$${R(x)}=(10+x)({100}-5x)$$
iii.
$${R(x)}=(10-5x)({100}+x)$$
iv.
$${R(x)}=(10+5x)({100}-x)$$
b.  At what price will the owner of the bakery earn the maximum weekly revenue from sales of the small cakes?

Problem 1
Set A and Set B each include a quadratic function represented as an equation, a graph, and a table.
Determine if all three representations in each set match. If not, explain how you would change one or more of the representations to make them match.
###IMAGE0###
###IMAGE1###
Problem 2
Which quadratic function below has the greatest positive value of
$$x$$
as a solution?
###IMAGE2###

Suppose Brett and Andre each throw a baseball into the air. The height of Brett's baseball is given by
$${h(t)=-16t^2+79t+6}$$
where
$$h$$
is in feet and
$$t$$
is in seconds. The height of Andre's baseball is given by the graph below.
###IMAGE0###
Brett claims that his baseball went higher than Andre’s, and Andre says that his baseball went higher.
Who is right?
How long is each baseball airborne?
Construct a graph of the height of Brett’s throw as a function of time on the same set of axes as the graph of Andre’s throw, and explain how this can confirm your claims to parts (a) and (b).

Problem 1
How do the following problems represent differences of two squares? Factor to show the relationships.
$${4x^4-1}$$
$${x^{10}-36}$$
Problem 2
Factor the following functions and name all of the real and complex zeros.
$${18x^4-32}$$

Find all of the roots of the following quartic equation:
$${h(x)=x^4-2x^3-9x^2-18x}$$

Problem 1
Below are three forms of the same quadratic function.
$${f(x)=-4(x-3)^2 +4}$$
$${f(x)=-4(x-2)(x-4)}$$
$${f(x)=-4x^2 +24x -32}$$
Without graphing, describe the features of the quadratic function.
Problem 2
Given the points below that represent the intercepts, vertex, and
y
-intercept, write the equation of the quadratic function in:
Standard form
Vertex form
Intercept form
$${(-5,1), (-4,0), (0,-24), (-6,0)}$$
Problem 3
Suppose
$$F = (0, 2)$$
and
$$l$$
is the the
$$x$$
-axis.
###IMAGE0###
Find the point on the
$$y$$
-axis equidistant from
$$F$$
and
$$l$$
. Label this point
$$P_0$$
.
Find the point on the line
$$x = 1$$
which is equidistant from
$$F$$
and
$$l$$
. Label this point
$$P_1$$
.
Find the point on the line
$$x = 2$$
which is equidistant from
$$F$$
and
$$l$$
. Label this point
$$P_2$$
.
If this process is repeated for the vertical lines
$$x = a$$
for all real numbers
$$a$$
, what curve do the points
$$P_a$$
make?

A theater decided to sell special event tickets at $10 per ticket to benefit a local charity. The theater can seat up to 1,000 people, and the manager of the theater expects to sell all 1,000 seats for the event. To maximize the revenue for this event, a research company volunteered to do a survey to find out whether the price of the ticket could be increased without losing revenue. The results showed that for each $1 increase in ticket price, 20 fewer tickets will be sold.
Let
$$x$$
represent the number of $1 price-per-ticket increases. Write an expression to represent the price for each ticket if there are
$$x$$
increases of $1.
Write an expression to represent the number of tickets sold if there are
$$x$$
increases of $1.
Write an expression to represent the revenue earned,
$$R$$
, from selling tickets with
$$x$$
increases of $1 per ticket.
How many $1 price-per-ticket increases will produce the maximum revenue?
What is the price per ticket that will provide the maximum revenue?
What is the maximum revenue?
How many tickets will the theater sell to reach the maximum revenue?
How much more will the theater make for the charity by using the higher ticket price than they would had they sold the tickets for their original price of $10 per ticket?

Problem 1
Find an equivalent rational expression in lowest terms, and identify the domain.
$${{{x^2-7x+12}\over{6-5x+x^2}}}$$
Problem 2
Determine whether or not the rational expressions
$${{x+4\over{(x+2)(x-3)}}}$$
and
$${{x^2+5x+4\over{(x+1)(x+2)(x-3)}}}$$
are equivalent for
$${x\neq -1}$$
,
$${x\neq -2}$$
, and
$${x\neq 3}$$
. Explain how you know.

Problem 1
Solve for the general solution:
$${3\mathrm{tan}^2x-3=0}$$
$${2\mathrm{cos}^2(x)-\sqrt3 \mathrm{cos}x=0}$$
$${2\mathrm{cos}^2x+\mathrm{cos}x-1=0}$$
Problem 2
Solve for the domain
$${0 \leq \theta \leq 2\pi}$$
:
$${2\mathrm{sin}^2\theta+\mathrm{cos}\theta\mathrm{sin}^2\theta=0}$$

Problem 1
Find the zeros of the function
$${f(x)=x^3-9x}$$
.
Problem 2
What are all of the linear factors of
$${2x^4-162}$$
? How would you write this expression in factored form?
Problem 3
How are the roots of a sum of two squares, such as
$${x^2+9}$$
, different from the roots of a difference of two squares?
What is the maximum area of a rectangle with a perimeter of 42 units?

Match each equation to its graph and table representation.
###IMAGE0###

Problem 1
Below are two quadratic functions with some additional information shown. Compare the features shown below.
###TABLE0###
Which function has a greater distance between the two roots?
Which function has a greater maximum value?
Which function has a greater rate of change from
$$ -4≤x≤0$$
?
Problem 2
The equation below expresses the approximate height,
h
, in meters of a ball,
t
seconds after it is launched from a pitching machine 3 feet off the ground with an initial velocity of
v
meters per second. If the batter doesn’t hit the ball, the ball will hit the ground in 8 seconds. What was the initial velocity of the ball?
$${h(t)=-4.9t^2+vt+3}$$

Problem 1
Suppose
$${h(t)=-5t^2+10t+3}$$
is the height of a diver above the water (in meters),
$$t$$
seconds after the diver leaves the springboard.
How high above the water is the springboard? Explain how you know.
When does the diver hit the water?
At what time on the diver's descent toward the water is the diver again at the same height as the springboard?
When does the diver reach the peak of the dive?
Problem 2
Chris stands on the edge of a building at a height of 60 feet. and throws a ball upward with an initial velocity of 68 feet per second. The ball eventually falls all the way to the ground.
Write a function to represent the height,
$$h$$
, of the ball after
$$t$$
seconds.
What is the maximum height reached by the ball? After how many seconds will it reach that height?
After how many seconds will the ball land on the ground after being thrown?
Sketch a graph of the motion of the ball. Label key features including the vertex,
$${y-}$$
intercept, and root(s).

The profit that a company makes selling an item (in thousands of dollars) depends on the price of the item (in dollars). If
$$p$$
is the price of the item, then three equivalent forms for the profit are:
Standard form:
$$-2p^2+24p-54$$
Factored form:
$$-2(p-3)(p-9)$$
Vertex form:
$$-2(p-6)^2+18$$
Which form is most useful for finding the prices that give a profit of zero dollars? Explain your reasoning and find the values.
Which form is most useful for finding the profit when the price is
$$0$$
? Explain your reasoning and find the value.
Which form is most useful for finding the price that gives the maximum profit? Explain your reasoning and find the value.

Task
Suppose $h(t) = -5t^2 + 10t + 3$ is the height of a diver above the water (in meters), $t$ seconds after the diver leaves the springboard.
How high above the water is the springboard? Explain how you know.
When does the diver hit the water?
At what time on the diver's descent toward the water is the diver again at the same height as the springboard?
When does the diver reach the peak of the dive?

Problem 1
For each equation below, use the zero product property to find all solutions. Explain each step in your reasoning.
a.
$${x(13-4x)=0}$$
b.
$${7(y+12)=0}$$
c.
$${(x-19)(x+3)=0}$$
d.
$${(y-6)(3z-4)=0}$$
Problem 2
Which equation represents the graph shown below? Explain your reasoning.
###IMAGE0###
a.
$${y=(x-3)(x+6)}$$
b.
$${y=(x+3)(x-6)}$$
c.
$${y=(x-3)(x-6)}$$
d.
$${y=(x+3)(x+6)}$$

Below is a quadratic equation in standard form and the associated graph and window settings.
###IMAGE0###
###IMAGE1###
###IMAGE2###
Find the vertex and the
y
-intercept algebraically.
Describe how you would find the vertex and the
y
-intercept on the graphing calculator.
Describe how you could adjust the graph so that the vertex, roots, and
y
-intercept are shown.

Task
Suppose $h(t) = -5t^2 + 10t + 3$ is the height of a diver above the water (in meters), $t$ seconds after the diver leaves the springboard.
How high above the water is the springboard? Explain how you know.
When does the diver hit the water?
At what time on the diver's descent toward the water is the diver again at the same height as the springboard?
When does the diver reach the peak of the dive?

Problem 1
Below are two functions:
$${f(x)=1}$$
$${g(x)=x}$$
Graph the quotient
$${{f(x)}\over{g(x)}}$$
. Name this function as
$${h(x).}$$
Problem 2
From what you know about transformations, what value(s) of
$${x }$$
cannot be part of the domain of the function:
$${g(x)={1\over{x-6}} }$$
?
Problem 3
What value(s) of
$$x$$
cannot be part of the domain of the function?
$$h(x)={{(x-3)(x+2)}\over{2(x-4)(x+2)}}$$

Problem 1
Below is a quadratic equation.
$${g(x)=4x^2+7x-15}$$
What are the roots, vertex, and
y
-intercept of this function?
Problem 2
Which equation matches with which graph? How do you know?
$${f(x)=-4x^2+8x-16}$$
$${g(x)=-4x^2+16}$$
###TABLE0###

Problem 1
Consider the equation
$${a\times b=0}$$
. What can you conclude about the values of
$$a$$
and
$$b$$
?
Consider the equation
$$(a+2)(b-1)=0$$
. What can you conclude about the values of
$$a$$
and
$$b$$
?
Problem 2
The solutions to a quadratic equation are given by the
$${x-}$$
intercepts of the graph. What are the solutions to the quadratic equation graphed below?
###IMAGE0###
The equation for the parabola shown is
$$y=({x-}3)(x+2)$$
. Show algebraically that the solutions to the quadratic equation are the same as those seen in the graph.
Problem 3
A quadratic function is shown in the graph below.
###IMAGE1###
The equation for this function is
$${y=(x+a)(2x+b)}$$
. What are possible values for
$$a$$
and
$$b$$
?

A softball player throws a ball with an initial velocity of 26 feet per second. The ball leaves her hand at 3 feet above the ground.
At the same time, her softball coach throws a ball with an initial velocity of 32 feet per second. The ball leaves her hand at 2 feet above the ground.
Whose ball takes longer to land on the ground after being thrown? By approximately how many seconds? Give your answer to the nearest tenth of a second.

Below is a quadratic equation with two missing values and a sketch of the graph of
$${f(x)}$$
.
$${f(x)}=r(x+2)^2 + m$$
###IMAGE0###
What do you know about the value of
$$r$$
and
$$m$$
based on the graph shown?
What do you know about the intercept form of the quadratic function?
Why can’t you write a unique quadratic equation based on this information?

Problem 1
Graph the following quadratic equation in your graphing calculator.
$${y=2x^2-4x+9}$$
Problem 2
Below is a quadratic equation written in standard form.
$${y=2x^2-4x+9}$$
And this is the graph of the quadratic equation on a graphing calculator.
###IMAGE0###
What do you need to change on the graphing calculator so you can “see” the
y
-intercept?
How can you use both the [ZOOM] and [WINDOW] features on the calculator to see this?
What options within the [CALC] feature will allow you to find the
y
-intercept?
Problem 3
Part A
Find the vertex of the quadratic equation using the graphing calculator.
$${y=2x^2-4x+9}$$
Alter the window so you can more clearly see the vertex.
Use the [CALC] feature to determine the minimum value.
Part B
The formula
$${-b\over2a}$$
can be used to find the axis of symmetry of the quadratic function. Use this to verify the vertex you found in the graphing calculator.

Problem 1
Find the roots of the function
$${f(x)=9x^3+36x^2-4x-16}$$
.
Problem 2
Find the roots of the function
$${g(x)=x^4-x^2-12}$$
.

Sketch a graph of the function below using the roots and the vertex.
$${f(x)=x^2+8x-20}$$
###IMAGE0###

The area of a rectangular storage room in square feet is given by the function
$${ A(x)=-2x^2+48x}$$
, where
$$x$$
represents the length of the room.
a.  Which inequality represents all of the possible lengths, in feet, of the storage room?
i.
$$0<x<12$$
ii.
$$12<x<24$$
iii.
$$0<x<24$$
iv.
$$x<0 \space \mathrm{or} \space x>24$$
b.  What length of the storage room will maximize the area of the room? What is the maximum area?

A toy company is manufacturing a new toy and trying to decide on a price that maximizes profit. The graph below represents profit,
$$P$$
, generated by each price of a toy,
$$x$$
. Answer the questions based on the graph of the quadratic function model.
###IMAGE0###
If the company wants to make a maximum profit, what should the price of a new toy be?
What is the minimum price of a toy that produces profit for the company? Explain your answer.
What does the point
$$P(10)=0$$
mean in context of the situation?
Find the domain that only results in a profit for the company and find its corresponding range of profit.

Problem 1
Jaden is selling tickets to a show his band is headlining. He knows from ticket sales for the last few shows they have done that their profits can be modeled by the function
$${P(x)=-{1\over16}x^2+24x+20}$$
, where
$$P$$
is the profit and
$$x$$
is the number of tickets sold.
How many tickets should Jaden sell so that he gets the maximum profit? What is that profit? What is the profit per ticket at this rate?
Problem 2
A family wants to fence in their yard and got 180 feet of fencing from a friend to do this project.
A diagram of their house and a sketch of the shape of the yard they want to fence in is shown below. The family is going to use the side of the house as one side of the yard.
###IMAGE0###
What are the dimensions of the yard that will give them the greatest fenced in area?

Task
Which of the following could be the function of a real variable $x$ whose graph is shown below? Explain.
###IMAGE0###
###TABLE0###

Problem 1
Use the quadratic equation below to answer the questions that follow.
$${y=x^2+2{x-}8 }$$
Find the roots and then plot them on a coordinate plane.
What equation represents the line of symmetry for this parabola?
What is the
$${x-}$$
coordinate of the vertex of the parabola?
What is the
$${y-}$$
coordinate of the vertex of the parabola?
Sketch the parabola in the coordinate plane.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
The
$${x-}$$
coordinate of the vertex can be found from the standard form of a quadratic equation using the formula
$${x=-{b\over2a}}$$
.
Find the roots and vertex of the quadratic equation below and use them to sketch a graph of the equation.
$$y=-3x^2+24{x-}36$$
Problem 3
Use the coordinate plane below to answer the questions that follow.
###IMAGE1###
a.  Write a quadratic equation that has the two points shown as solutions.
b.  Find the vertex of the equation you wrote and then sketch the graph of the parabola.

Solve the following equations for all values
$${0\leq x\leq2\pi}$$
.
$${3\mathrm{sin}\theta+2\mathrm{sin}^2\theta=-1}$$
$${\mathrm{sin}\theta\mathrm{cos}\theta-{1\over2}\mathrm{sin}\theta=0}$$
